title: Bridge Essay: Neuroimaging in Functional Movement Disorders.
concept: Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Free will — sense of agency, voluntary action, intentional binding, volitional control.

**Neuroimaging in Functional Movement Disorders.** - Curr Neurol Neurosci Rep (2019). DOI: 10.1007/s11910-019-0926-y. PMID: 30747347. PURPOSE OF REVIEW: Functional movement disorders are common and disabling causes of abnormal movement control. Here, we review the current state of the evidence on the use of neuroimaging in Functional movement disorders, particularly its role in helping to unravel the pathophysiology of this enigmatic condition.
